officialConfigValueSource.snippet: MS<Bool>("general:allow_tearing", "master switch for allowing tearing to occur.", false),

officialUsageSourceHits.snippet: Log::logger->log(Log::WARN, "Tearing commit requested but the master switch general:allow_tearing is off, ignoring");
MS<Bool>("general:allow_tearing", "master switch for allowing tearing to occur.", false),

officialWikiHits.snippet: | allow_tearing | master switch for allowing tearing to occur. See [the Tearing page](../../Advanced-and-Cool/Tearing). | bool | `false` |

allow_tearing = true

companionSchemaEvidence.description: Allow screen tearing for reduced latency

officialConfigValueSource.snippet: MS<Int>("misc:vrr", "controls the VRR (Adaptive Sync) of your monitors", 0, {.min = 0, .max = 3, .map = OptionMap{{"off", 0}, {"on", 1}, {"fullscreen", 2}, {"fullscreen_game", 3}}}),

officialUsageSourceHits.snippet: MS<Int>("misc:vrr", "controls the VRR (Adaptive Sync) of your monitors", 0,

officialWikiHits.snippet: | no_vrr | boolean | Disables VRR for the window. Only works when `misc.vrr` is set to `2` or `3`. |
| vrr | controls the VRR (Adaptive Sync) of your monitors. 0 - off, 1 - on, 2 - fullscreen only, 3 - fullscreen with `video` or `game` content type [0/1/2/3] | int | `0` |

| no_break_fs_vrr | disables scheduling new frames on cursor movement for fullscreen apps with VRR enabled to avoid framerate spikes (may require no_hardware_cursors = true) 0 - off, 1 - on, 2 - auto (on with content type 'game') | int | `2
| min_refresh_rate | minimum refresh rate for cursor movement when `no_break_fs_vrr` is active. Set to minimum supported refresh rate or higher | int | `24` |

companionSchemaEvidence.description: Controls adaptive sync globally

officialConfigValueSource.snippet: MS<Bool>("xwayland:enabled", "allow running applications using X11", true),

officialUsageSourceHits.snippet: MS<Bool>("xwayland:enabled", "allow running applications using X11", true),

officialWikiHits.snippet: Some applications provide native systemd user units to be autostarted with. Those might need to be enabled explicitly via `systemctl --user enable [some-app.service]`. Or, in case the unit is missing `[Install]` section, enabled more direct
installed, enabled and running if you don't have them yet.
USB debugging is enabled.
As of Nvidia driver version 570.86.16, `fbdev` has now been enabled by default

companionSchemaEvidence.description: Allow running applications using X11

officialConfigValueSource.snippet: MS<Bool>("debug:overlay", "print the debug performance overlay.", false),

officialWikiHits.snippet: When triggered, `cursor-clip` will automatically position its overlay window at your current mouse location, providing a Windows 11-style clipboard history interface. The overlay supports all clipboard formats including text, images, and fi
The `default` Hyprland overlay only contains the Hyprland package along with xdg-desktop-portal-hyprland, and Hyprland's internal dependencies (udis86-hyprland and glaze-hyprland).
This means you need to import all the overlays for the hypr* dependencies yourself if you want them up to date. Otherwise Hyprland will build with the versions available in Nixpkgs.
If you instead want an overlay with all dependencies, import both `hyprland-packages` and `hyprland-extras` overlays.

companionSchemaEvidence.description: print the debug performance overlay.

rows.notes: Wiki documents finite values and MasterAlgorithm.cpp only branches on left/right/top, with all other strings falling through to bottom behavior. App validator should reject arbitrary strings.

rows.notes: Hyprland parser accepts arbitrary string config, but ScrollingAlgorithm.cpp logs invalid float tokens and falls back if no valid widths remain. App validator should reject non-float tokens.
rows.allowedValueShape: comma-separated floats, each parsed with std::stof and clamped to MIN_COLUMN_WIDTH/MAX_COLUMN_WIDTH by source; empty/invalid entries fall back or are logged.

officialConfigValueSource.snippet: MS<Int>("quirks:prefer_hdr", "Prefer HDR mode.", 0, {.min = 0, .max = 2, .map = OptionMap{{"disable", 0}, {"enable", 1}, {"gamescope_only", 2}}}),

officialWikiHits.snippet: | prefer_hdr | Report HDR mode as preferred. 0 - off, 1 - always, 2 - gamescope only | int | `0` |
Some clients expect monitor to be in HDR mode prior to the client start. This breaks auto HDR activation and can cause whitescreen and flickering. Use `prefer_hdr` to fix it.

companionSchemaEvidence.description: Prefer HDR mode.
